name: Soy bean (food medium)
basis: per 100 g raw mature soybean seed (fresh weight, ~8.5% moisture)

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: dominant macronutrient; source of amino-acid pool below

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: largely insoluble cell-wall polysaccharide; not readily fermentable by most models

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 Crop J, ScienceDirect S2214514122001192 (n=1164 accessions); consistent with USDA total sugars 7.3 g
components.evidence: dominant low-molecular-weight free sugar in mature soybean seed

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 Crop J S2214514122001192

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 Crop J S2214514122001192

components.name: stachyose
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 Crop J S2214514122001192 (mean 35.47 mg/g)
components.evidence: major raffinose-family oligosaccharide (RFO); fermentable to some microbes via alpha-galactosidase

components.name: raffinose
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 Crop J S2214514122001192 (mean 6.60 mg/g)

components.name: verbascose
components.evidence: minor RFO present but typically <0.2 g/100g; not reliably quantified here

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: anchors the individual-sugar sum above (~6.8 g)

components.name: malic acid (L-malate)
components.source: HPLC identification in soybean seed (Zhu et al. NMR study doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1); magnitude reference Song et al. 2013 Ind Crops Prod S0926669013004810 (organic acids 3.75-6.75 mg/g DM, malate dominant, in vegetable/immature soybean)
components.evidence: present and typically the dominant organic acid; not reliably quantified for mature dry seed (mature seed lower than the immature edamame reference). Already in current components as L-Malate

components.name: citric acid
components.source: HPLC soybean-seed identification (S2212429224018133 reports citric predominant in soy hulls 0.99 g/100g)
components.evidence: present in seed; not quantified for whole mature seed

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.evidence: dominant organic P store; ~50-70% of seed total phosphorus; chelates minerals

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: limiting sulfur amino acid in soy

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: USDA lists L-serine; current record's D-serine is likely a mis-stereochemistry

components.name: vitamin A
components.evidence: negligible; from carotenoids, not preformed retinol

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: gamma-tocopherol is actually the dominant tocopherol in soy oil

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: dominant fatty acid; current record '(Z,Z)-9,12-Octadecadienoic acid'

components.name: phosphatidylcholine
components.source: FooDB FOOD00085; soy lecithin ~1.5-2% of seed
components.evidence: genuine soy phospholipid (lecithin); present but not quantified here

remove.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid
remove.reason: marine omega-3 (20:5 n-3); absent from soybean oil (USDA reports none). FooDB predicted/implausible

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed (animal) vitamin A; plants contain provitamin-A carotenoids, not retinol

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: vitamin B12; not synthesized by plants, USDA lists 0 ug in soybean

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element; not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant; not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element; not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Titanium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant; not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Gadoleic acid
remove.reason: 20:1 gondoic acid; only ultratrace in soybean oil, not a meaningful nutrient

remove.name: Heptadecanoic acid
remove.reason: odd-chain 17:0; ultratrace, ruminant/dairy marker, not a soybean nutrient of note

remove.name: Pentadecanoic acid
remove.reason: odd-chain 15:0; ultratrace dairy marker, not a soybean nutrient of note

remove.name: Dodecanoic acid
remove.reason: lauric 12:0; not present at meaningful levels in soybean oil

remove.name: Heptanoate
remove.reason: C7 fatty acid; not a genuine soybean-seed component (FooDB noise)

remove.name: Maltose C12H22O11
remove.reason: not a native free sugar of raw soybean seed (arises only on malting/hydrolysis); soy free sugars are sucrose + RFOs

remove.name: Starch C12H20O10
remove.reason: mature soybean seed stores oil/protein, not starch (<1 g/100g); negligible for growth

missing_added: individual free sugars with amounts (sucrose 4.45, glucose 0.43, fructose 0.21 g/100g)
organic acids (malic dominant, plus citric, succinic, oxalic, fumaric) added qualitatively
full vitamin panel with amounts (B1-B9, C, A, E, K, choline)
major fatty acids with amounts (linoleic dominant)
keep_uncovered: genistein / daidzein / glycitein and their glucosides & malonyl/acetyl conjugates (genistin, daidzin, glycitin, 6''-malonyl/acetyl forms) - genuine soy isoflavones, no BiGG met
beta-sitosterol, campesterol, stigmasterol - genuine soy phytosterols
soyasaponin I / saponins - genuine soy triterpenoid glycosides
D-pinitol, galactopinitol B, ciceritol - genuine soy cyclitols/oligos
syringic / sinapic / ferulic / gallic / vanillic / 4-hydroxybenzoic acids - genuine phenolic acids
notes: Raw mature soybean seed. Dominant carbon/energy sources for a growth model: sucrose (~4.5 g/100g) plus the RFOs stachyose (~1.3) and raffinose (~0.3), the large amino-acid pool (glutamate + aspartate dominant, total protein 36.5 g/100g), and oil fatty acids (linoleic dominant). Free glucose/fructose are minor. Organic acids are present (malate dominant) but low and poorly quantified for mature dry seed, so listed with null amounts. Phytic acid sequesters much of the phosphorus and chelates minerals. Sugar values from Zhang et al. 2022 (n=1164) reconcile with USDA total sugars 7.3 g.
sources: USDA FDC 174270 (Soybeans, mature seeds, raw; SR Legacy NDB 16108)
Zhang et al. 2022, The Crop Journal, seed soluble-sugar profiling of 1164 accessions (ScienceDirect S2214514122001192)
Song et al. 2013, Industrial Crops and Products (vegetable-soybean sugar/AA/organic-acid, S0926669013004810)
Zhu et al. 2020, Food Prod Process Nutr, doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1 (organic acids by 1H NMR)
